Charlanne.
Brand strategist & designer Prague — available worldwide
00 — Director's note 00

I spent eight years
making sure nothing
went wrong. Now I make
sure everything feels right.

I came to design from fintech compliance, years translating regulation into human behavior, finding where systems broke against people. Turns out that's exactly what brand strategy is. Find the friction. Name the truth. Build around it.

I don't manufacture brand narratives. I excavate them: the genuine human thing inside a business that, once named, makes everything else obvious.

American. Prague-based since 2013. Moravian by marriage.

Selected work 05 specimens
01
Brand Identity · Hospitality
Bad Bun
"Rebellion is just integrity with better taste in music."
+
The work
The brief

Burger brand for an independent operator tired of artisan-everything. Not fast food. Not fine dining. The gap between them: where attitude lives.

Human truth found

Counter-culture food branding is usually performed rebellion, a safe brand pretending to be edgy. The real truth here was pride without permission. Bad Bun doesn't ask you to validate it. It doesn't explain the brass knuckles. It already knows it's good.

Decisions made — and killed
→Used blackletter not because it's "edgy" but because blackletter encodes authority: old-world craft confidence. Killed the distressed texture version: distressed = insecure.
→Acid yellow as primary, not accent. Yellow is warning signs and taxi cabs, things you can't ignore. Killed deep red: reads as fast food.
→Brass knuckle mark kept abstract enough to read as a B at first glance. The double-read is the point: you see what you're ready to see.
Palette
Disciplines
IdentityTypographyStrategyPackaging
What this proves
A brand with a POV so clear every decision follows from it. No trend-chasing.
02
Brand Identity · Restaurant
Gogiya
"The table is a teacher. Every grandmother knows this."
+
The work
The brief

Korean BBQ restaurant needing an identity that felt neither Western-appropriated nor like a caricature. Warmth that wasn't saccharine. Heritage that wasn't a museum piece.

Human truth found

Korean BBQ is a format for being together. The grill in the center means nobody can hide. Nobody eats alone. The grandmother figure emerged not as mascot but as keeper of table law.

Decisions made — and killed
→Grandmother illustrated, not photographed. Photographs age. Illustrations become mythic. Killed the smiling version: she's approving, not performing warmth.
→No hanbok clichés. Visual language rooted in ceramics and lacquerware, objects that hold things, passed down.
→Social content: table etiquette as axioms. Specific enough to be real, universal enough to land outside Korean culture.
Palette
Disciplines
IdentityIllustrationSocialNaming
What this proves
Cross-cultural research without appropriation. Finding the universal inside the specific.
03
UX Design · SaaS Tool
Surfaux
"Most UX problems are trust problems in disguise."
+
The work
Images coming soon
Images coming soon
Images coming soon
The brief

Self-initiated. Build an AI-powered UX audit tool proving technical range, not just design taste, but product thinking and the ability to ship.

Human truth found

UX audits are expensive because they require an expert to translate what they see into what a stakeholder will act on. The real problem is communicating issues to someone who didn't design the thing.

Decisions made — and killed
→Used Lovable for dev, an intentional choice to demonstrate AI-assisted product work as a design skill, not a shortcut.
→Output modeled on compliance reports: severity tiers, remediation priority, plain-language summaries. Killed the version that read like a design critique: wrong audience.
→Audits any public URL with zero setup. Killed the authenticated flow because onboarding friction defeats the point.
Built with
Lovable · Claude API · custom prompting
Disciplines
UXProductAIDev
What this proves
I can ship. I think in systems. I know which audience I'm designing for at every decision point.
04
Personal Brand · Career Pivot
The Pivot
"Every system I ever audited was a design problem."
+
The work
Images coming soon
Images coming soon
Images coming soon
The brief

Reposition eight years of fintech compliance as a design differentiator, not baggage. Build a personal brand for someone who thinks differently from most designers because she came from somewhere they didn't.

Human truth found

Most designers learn to see what works. Compliance trained me to see where things break against human behavior. The pivot is the discovery that logic and creativity were the same thing all along, wearing different uniforms.

Decisions made — and killed
→Lead with the compliance background everywhere. Hiding it reads as shame. Foregrounding it reads as confidence.
→LinkedIn voice: dry humor, systems thinking, anti-corporate. Killed the inspirational pivot narrative: this story is about what compliance teaches you that design school doesn't.
→Target independent operators and almost-famous founders who need brand thinking but can't afford a big agency.
The one-liner
"I design for humanity. I learned how from compliance."
Skills mapped
Risk mapping → friction analysis
Regulatory translation → brand clarity
Audit reports → UX documentation
05
Brand Identity · Non-Alcoholic Drinks
Úplná Nula
"Sobriety shouldn't look like punishment."
+
The work
The brief

Brand identity for a Czech non-alcoholic drinks brand. 0% alcohol, with no compromise on the ritual, the occasion, or the visual register. This needed to live on a terrace in summer, at a New Year's table, in a wine bar. Without apology.

Human truth found

The non-alcoholic category tends to compensate: it over-explains, adds too many wellness cues, tries to justify itself. The real opportunity was refusing to compensate at all. Úplná Nula (Czech for "absolute zero") treats sobriety as a choice with taste, not a deprivation with a badge.

Decisions made — and killed
→Typography at full bleed, overlaid on product photography. Puts the brand on the same visual plane as the drink: equal footing, not labeling.
→The ú/n mark in a sealed oval, referencing liquor stamps, pharmacy seals, certification marks. Things that imply quality by association. Killed the wordmark-only version: needed something iconic at small sizes.
→Warm amber + pale blue palette. Amber carries the warmth of spirits without being a copy. Blue is clarity, coolness, restraint. Killed green: reads wellness and kombucha: wrong territory entirely.
→Tagline "0% Alcohol. 100% Taste.", direct, no poetry. The typography makes it editorial. Killed the poetic version: this brand earns its elegance through confidence, not metaphor.
Palette
Disciplines
Identity Typography Photography Strategy Naming
What this proves
I can do restrained and elegant. Taste is knowing which register the brand lives in.
Let's find the truth in yours
